``` ::: ::: ::: {.panel-tabset .nav-pills} # Parole Eligibility ## How Parole Eligibility is Determined With no parole release, reductions in time served are only through good time credit which depends on incarcerated people's classification into one of several tiers. Day for day credit is the maximum tier for a 50% discount but educational credit may further increase the discount.
## In Prison Past Their Parole Eligibility Year In 2020, there were 655 people in prison past their parole eligibility year. This group made up 4% of people in prison for new crimes and with sentence lengths between 1 to 25 years. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ### Demographics The demographics of people in prison past their parole eligibility year reveal notable proportions among White, non-Hispanic and black, non-hispanic people. Gender distribution indicates a predominance of males over females. Age-wise, the majority of people were 25-34 years and 35-44 years old. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ### Criminal Offenses ::: {.cell} ::: In 2020, 69% of people in prison past their parole eligibility year were in prison for non-violent offenses. The breakdown of criminal offenses of people in prison past their parole eligibility year reveals a varied landscape, with the majority of people incarcerated for property (27%) and drugs (23%) offenses. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ### Original Sentence Lengths In 2020, among the prison population eligible for parole but not yet released, people with sentences between 2 to 4.9 years constituted the majority, representing 36%. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Missing Data This section will contain information related to missingness in the NCRP data. Keep?


# Prison Population ## Prison Population Trends From 2010 to 2022, the prison population decreased 11%, changing from 28,028 in 2010 to 24,972 in 2022. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: Source: BJS Prisoners Series
## Demographics ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: Source: BJS Prisoners 2022 - Race and Ethnicity

``` ::: :::
## Sentence Lengths ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
# Prison Releases ## Prison Release Trends From 2010 to 2020, prison releases decreased 49%, dropping from 19,929 in 2010 to 10,121 in 2020. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
In 2020, 52% of people eligible for parole were released during their eligibility year. This represents a 44% decrease compared to 2010. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Conditional Releases Conditional release involves an individual’s release under specific conditions and supervision, whereas unconditional release means the individual is released without further obligations or restrictions. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Demographics of People Released The demographics of people released from prison reveal notable proportions among White, non-Hispanic and black, non-hispanic people. Gender distribution indicates a predominance of males over females. Age-wise, the majority of people were 25-34 years and 35-44 years old. These findings provide insights into the populations transitioning back into the community. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Time Served by Offense Between 2010 and 2020, there were shifts in the average time served by individuals for different offense types in Indiana. The largest change was for property offenses, which increased by 107%. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
# Disparities ## Incarceration Rates In Indiana, Black, non-Hispanic people are incarcerated in state prison at a rate 4.7 times higher than White, non-Hispanic people, when accounting for population sizes in the community. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Years Spent in Prison After Parole Eligibility ::: {.panel-tabset .custom-tabs} ### Race and Ethnicity In 2020, people in prison past their parole eligibility year collectively spent a total of 379 years behind bars instead of supervised in the community on parole. This extended incarceration varied across different racial and ethnic groups.

## Timing of Release After Parole Eligibility ::: {.panel-tabset .custom-tabs} ### Race and Ethnicity The timing of release for individuals after their parole eligibility year varied by offense type and demographic group, with many remaining in prison for several years beyond their eligibility date. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ### Gender :::
## Time Served ::: {.panel-tabset .custom-tabs} ### Race and Ethnicity ::: {.cell} ::: Hispanic, any race individuals faced the longest average time served in prison in 2020, with an average of 3.4 years. Other race(s), non-Hispanic individuals experienced shorter prison stays, averaging 2.1 years. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: By offense type, disparities were observed in time served by race and ethnicity. For Murder and Non-negligent Manslaughter offenses, Hispanic, any race individuals had 11.6 more years on average compared to Other race(s), non-Hispanic individuals, who had the shortest time served for these offenses. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ### Gender :::
